b2-vix-reversion.name: b2-vix-reversion
b2-vix-reversion.mechanism: Volatility spikes overshoot because protection is bought at any price; when VIX closes far above its recent norm vol sellers re-enter and equities rebound next session

scan-mcl.name: mcl-vix (crude)
scan-mcl.mechanism: MARKET-EXPANSION SCAN of MCL (micro WTI crude): the five validated mechanism configs (monday, nr7, prehol, vix-rebound, open-drive) run natively; each market is judged on its own merits per Daniel's scope-opening order — no cross-market consistency required, and every config counted as a trial

scan-mbt.name: mbt-nr7 (bitcoin)
scan-mbt.mechanism: MARKET-EXPANSION SCAN of MBT (micro bitcoin): the five validated mechanism configs (monday, nr7, prehol, vix-rebound, open-drive) run natively; each market is judged on its own merits per Daniel's scope-opening order — no cross-market consistency required, and every config counted as a trial

scan-mym.name: mym-prehol (Dow)
scan-mym.mechanism: MARKET-EXPANSION SCAN of MYM (micro Dow): the five validated mechanism configs (monday, nr7, prehol, vix-rebound, open-drive) run natively; each market is judged on its own merits per Daniel's scope-opening order — no cross-market consistency required, and every config counted as a trial

b3-nr7-breakout.name: b3-nr7-breakout
b3-nr7-breakout.mechanism: Volatility compression precedes expansion; a break out of the narrowest range in 7 days carries real information

b10-nagel-vix-reversal.name: b10-nagel-vix-reversal
b10-nagel-vix-reversal.mechanism: Short-horizon reversal is the return to liquidity provision and spikes when intermediaries retreat (high VIX) — you are paid to absorb urgency when it is scarcest (Nagel RFS 2012)

b3-monday-long.name: b3-monday-long
b3-monday-long.mechanism: Weekend risk premium and Monday retail inflows favor longs at the week open; the shelf's Monday sleeve has never been honestly measured on real data

b3-preholiday-drift.name: b3-preholiday-drift
b3-preholiday-drift.mechanism: Documented pre-holiday drift: shorts cover and liquidity providers mark up ahead of exchange closures

b4-open-drive.name: b4-open-drive
b4-open-drive.mechanism: An open-drive (three one-directional opening bars closing at the extreme) is initiative institutional flow that does not revert intraday

rep-bondflight-mes.name: rep-bondflight-mes
rep-bondflight-mes.mechanism: Flight-to-quality unwind is a market-wide equity phenomenon and must appear on MES if the treasury fear gauge is real

b2-opex-release.name: b2-opex-release
b2-opex-release.mechanism: Monthly options expiry pins price to dealer gamma into early afternoon; when hedges roll off the pin releases and the afternoon break trends into the close

b3-tom-long.name: b3-tom-long
b3-tom-long.mechanism: Pension/401k inflows cluster at month boundaries and are price-insensitive buyers; long the turn-of-month sessions

fomc-statement-presser-reversal.name: fomc-statement-presser-reversal
fomc-statement-presser-reversal.mechanism: FOMC releases information in two stages: the 14:00 ET statement, then the 14:30 ET press conference. The 14:00-14:30 move is priced off statement text alone by fast algorithmic readers with no chair commentary, and the presser routinely revises that reading, so the first-stage move is systematically over-extended relative to the full information set. We are paid by whoever had to take the first-stage risk.

b3-prior-high-break.name: b3-prior-high-break
b3-prior-high-break.mechanism: Stops cluster above the prior-day high; the first break runs them and continues

rep-nagel-mes.name: rep-nagel-mes
rep-nagel-mes.mechanism: Liquidity-provision reversal premium is market-wide and must appear on MES when VIX is elevated

b6-monday-m2k.name: b6-monday-m2k
b6-monday-m2k.mechanism: Weekend equity risk premium should extend to small-caps (Russell): Monday-long on M2K

b6-vix-m2k.name: b6-vix-m2k
b6-vix-m2k.mechanism: VIX fear-overshoot rebounds should extend to Russell since small-caps are typically MORE sensitive to risk-appetite swings

rep3-vix-mes.name: rep3-vix-mes
rep3-vix-mes.mechanism: VIX fear-overshoot is market-wide: must appear on MES

b3-lunch-compression-break.name: b3-lunch-compression-break
b3-lunch-compression-break.mechanism: Lunch lull compresses range; afternoon institutional re-engagement breaks it with real flow

b4-bigday-fade.name: b4-bigday-fade
b4-bigday-fade.mechanism: A single-day move beyond 1.5% overshoots via forced flows and margin selling; next-open liquidity providers fade it

decisions.title: Book sleeve mcl-vix (scan-mcl) failed robustness — pull from the book?
decisions.recommendation: REMOVE from the book — plateau + wrong-date placebo failures on formal hardening
decisions.evidence: Robustness battery on MCL scan family: vix-rebound-long fails parameter plateau and wrong-date placebo; monday/prehol variants also fail (ledger, stage=robustness, 2026-07-22)
decisions.what_yes_means: Book drops another sleeve; sizing and all bundles recompute without mcl-vix
decisions.recap: Same situation as mym-prehol: a scan-campaign sleeve that never faced the formal battery just did, and its edge doesn't separate from placebo. Decide whether it keeps its book slot.
decisions.why: The crude VIX-rebound sleeve's profits move when its dates are displaced and its edge lives on a narrow parameter setting — the two classic signatures of a fitted artifact rather than a mechanism. It was carrying 1 micro in every current book; that allocation would go to sleeves that survived.
steps: Approve: DEMOTED stands; scan-mcl leaves the validated book set
Sizing + bundles recompute on the remaining sleeves
Dashboard book/roadmap views update on next publish
History stays on the ledger; re-validation can reinstate later
Deny: sleeve keeps its slot; the robustness result remains recorded either way

decisions.title: Book sleeve mym-prehol (scan-mym) failed robustness — pull from the book?
decisions.recommendation: REMOVE from the book — mechanism placebo failed on formal hardening
decisions.evidence: Robustness battery on MYM scan family: prehol-long fails mechanism controls; monday-long fails mechanism + ex-best-year; nr7-orb fails ex-best-year (ledger, stage=robustness, 2026-07-22)
decisions.what_yes_means: Book drops from 8 sleeves to 7; sizing and all bundles recompute without mym-prehol
decisions.recap: Your book's MYM pre-holiday sleeve was validated in the original scan campaign but had never been through the formal robustness battery. Tonight it was — and its mechanism didn't survive placebo testing. This card decides whether it stays in the book.
decisions.why: The scan-* sleeves entered the book from the market-native scan campaign with lighter checks than the b3/b4 sleeves. The battery that just demoted ten pretenders applied the same standard here and the pre-holiday edge on MYM looks indistinguishable from placebo dates. Keeping a placebo-grade sleeve means its micros and breach budget are spent on noise.
steps: Approve: DEMOTED verdict stands; scan-mym leaves the validated book set
gauntlet sizing + all bundles recompute on the 7 remaining sleeves (numbers drop honestly)
The dashboard book/roadmap views update on the next publish
Trade logs and history stay on disk; a future re-validation can reinstate it
Deny: sleeve stays in the book; the robustness result stays on the ledger either way

decisions.title: Sign off: demote lunch-break + bigday-fade (overlap claim retracted)
decisions.recommendation: DEMOTE both — lunch on failed MES replication, not on overlap; your veto pending
decisions.evidence: lunch-break MES replication FAILED (PF 0.926, net -$3,278, n=1,357) + morning-control drift + 2022 concentration; bigday-fade t=0.42 at n=202, MES PF 1.008, ex-2020 MES net -$4,060. Overlap-with-monday evidence RETRACTED: weekly r=0.028, 50% sign agreement, combined maxDD -$1,794 < either sleeve alone.
decisions.what_yes_means: Both leave the candidate pool (reversible via ledger)
decisions.recap: Moves lunch-break and bigday-fade out of the candidate pool. They stop being considered for bundles and future books. Nothing is deleted. The original overlap argument against lunch-break was tested and withdrawn — the demotion now rests only on its failed MES replication.
decisions.why: Your question was right and it changed this card. I measured the overlap on dev-era data: lunch-break and monday-long share 137 weeks, but their weekly P&L correlation is 0.028, they agree in direction 50% of the time, and lunch made +$2,371 across the 60 weeks monday lost. Together their max drawdown is -$1,794 — smaller than lunch alone (-$3,048) or monday alone (-$2,354). That is diversification, not double-counting, so the overlap evidence is retracted. Overlap only ever matters as a book-risk argument — sleeves are sized off the book's drawdown, so sleeves that lose in the same weeks stack into one breach — and it does not apply here. What does still stand: lunch-break fails MES replication outright (PF 0.926, -$3,278 over 1,357 trades), its morning-window control kept a positive drift (so part of it is generic range-break momentum, not a lunch-specific mechanism), and $6,436 of its $10,603 came from 2022 with 2023 negative. Bigday-fade is untouched by the question: mean $10.05 on sd $337 at n=202 is t=0.42, MES PF 1.008, ex-2020 MES net -$4,060 — a 2020-crash artifact.
steps: DEMOTED verdicts are appended to the ledger for both strategies (append-only — history stays intact)
The lunch-break verdict cites the failed MES replication, NOT overlap; the retracted overlap claim and its numbers (r=0.028, combined maxDD -$1,794) are recorded alongside it
Their leaderboard tier drops from candidate to demoted; rows dim
They are excluded from the bundle pool and every future sizing run
Trade logs and all past results are kept on disk untouched
If new evidence ever clears them — e.g. lunch-break replicating on a fresh instrument — a fresh ledger entry reinstates; nothing is irreversible
Dashboard republishes on the next cycle

qa.q: Why does it matter if it overlaps if it's its own strategy?
qa.a:
You were right to push back — I measured it and the overlap claim does not hold. On the dev era (2022-03-01..2025-02-28) lunch-break and monday-long share 137 active weeks, but their weekly P&L correlation is r=0.028 and they agree on direction in only 50% of those weeks — a coin flip. Lunch actually made +$2,371 across the 60 weeks monday LOST money (avg +$40/wk, versus +$15/wk in monday's winning weeks). Run together, the book's max drawdown is -$1,794 — smaller than either sleeve alone (lunch -$3,048, monday -$2,354). Calendar overlap is not the same thing as edge overlap, and I have retracted that evidence line.

Why overlap matters at all, when it is real: a sleeve is sized off the BOOK's drawdown, not its own. Two sleeves that lose in the same weeks stack their losses into a single breach while the blended backtest still looks smooth — that was the down-weeks lesson. It is always a book-risk argument, never an argument that the strategy is invalid on its own. Here the numbers say these two are genuinely uncorrelated, so the argument does not apply.

Lunch-break's demotion still stands, on the reason that actually survives: it FAILED MES replication — PF 0.926, net -$3,278 over n=1,357, i.e. it loses money on the second instrument. Add the morning-window control that kept a positive drift (so part of it is generic range-break momentum, not a lunch-specific mechanism) and the 2022 concentration ($6,436 of $10,603 from 2022, with 2023 negative). Three independent strikes on the mechanism.

Bigday-fade is unchanged by your question: n=202, mean $10.05/trade on sd $337 — t=0.42, indistinguishable from zero; MES PF 1.008 and ex-2020 MES net -$4,060, so its edge is substantially a 2020-crash artifact.

decisions.title: Apex verification APPROVED — paste the five numbers
decisions.recommendation: Send the 5 verified Apex-150K values; the engine cannot read Apex (bot-blocked)
decisions.evidence: Approved 2026-07-21. No numbers came with the approval, so the Apex-150K model is still ASSUMED: start 150,000 / target 159,000 / EOD trailing buffer 4,000 / floor freezes at 150,000 (114_apex_sim.py). Profit split, consistency % and max-accounts are not modelled at all — there is no apex150 plan in gauntlet/account.py.
decisions.what_yes_means: Numbers land -> engine builds a VERIFIED apex150 plan and re-runs all Apex sizings
decisions.recap: You said yes to verifying Apex. The engine cannot open Apex's site itself, so it is holding here until you paste the five values from your own login.
decisions.why: Approving the check does not change any math on its own — every Apex number on this site is still the assumption set from 114_apex_sim.py, and the split / consistency / max-accounts rules were never modelled. 18 of the 23 accounts in the $10k route depend on those values. Rather than silently promote assumptions to 'verified', the engine keeps one open card until the real numbers arrive.
steps: You read off Apex's site, for the 150K account: (1) trailing threshold amount in $, (2) whether trailing is intraday or end-of-day, (3) payout consistency % rule, (4) profit split %, (5) max accounts allowed per person
Paste those five values into the Approve box on this card
Engine adds a verified 'apex150' plan to gauntlet/account.py PLANS using exactly those rules
All Apex sizings, bundles and first-payout estimates re-run against the verified plan
ASSUMED chips are removed only for the fields you supply; anything skipped stays flagged ASSUMED

qa.q: Approve: Apex account verification before any funding
qa.a: Approved by Daniel 2026-07-21. Approval recorded in ledger.jsonl; no rule values were included, so no Apex number was changed. Card replaced with this one, which holds until the five values arrive.

decisions.title: Slippage collector is built — but the bot places no orders
decisions.recommendation: Give the GO (and Tradovate demo creds) to route paper orders, or the sample count stays 0
decisions.evidence: Repurpose approved 2026-07-21 and the machinery is live: scheduler/slippage_collector.py logs the backtest-assumed fill prices at arm and pairs them with actual broker fills at flatten (scheduler/slippage.jsonl). Hand-verified on synthetic fills. BUT scheduler/.env has MNQ_LIVE=0 and all four TRADOVATE_* credentials blank, so the bot sends nothing and there is no actual fill to measure. Separately, the 09:30-09:45 OR levels are not wired (Tradovate market data is a WebSocket feed); arming needs ORB_OR_HI/ORB_OR_LO set by hand.
decisions.what_yes_means: Paper orders start flowing on the Tradovate DEMO account and real fill samples accumulate
decisions.recap: The measuring machinery is finished and hooked into the bot. It just has nothing to measure yet: the bot is in notify-only mode with no broker login, so it never actually places the paper orders whose fills we wanted to compare.
decisions.why: The whole point of approving the repurpose was to test the assumption that backtest fills are close to real fills. A collector that logs 'no broker' every day tests nothing. Rather than flip the bot to order-sending mode on my own — your standing order is that no paper or live routing starts without your GO — the engine stops here and asks. Two things are missing: your GO plus Tradovate demo credentials, and a way to get the opening-range levels to the bot (either wire the market-data feed or paste the two numbers daily).
steps: You approve and put Tradovate DEMO credentials in scheduler/.env (TRADOVATE_USER/PASS/CID/SEC, TRADOVATE_DEMO=1)
Engine runs orb_intraday_bot.py --test-connection first (auth + account lookup, no orders placed)
Only if that passes: MNQ_LIVE=1, so the bot sends its usual paper orders to the demo account
Each arm freezes the assumed fill prices; each flatten pulls the actual fills and logs the difference
At ~100 samples the bot retires for good and the measured slippage replaces the assumed cost model
Deny instead and the bot shuts down now with 0 slippage samples collected

qa.q: Approve: Neo-300 paper bot — retire or repurpose as slippage collector
qa.a: Approved by Daniel 2026-07-21. Repurpose executed: the bot's strategy is flagged dead and its signals are no longer to be acted on; slippage_collector.py is built, hand-verified and wired into arm/flatten. Blocker found on execution: MNQ_LIVE=0 and no Tradovate credentials, so 0 fills can be observed. This card holds for the GO to route paper orders.

roadmap.disclaimer: All figures simulation-tier until live fills.
